ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic croissant entry (FOODON_03301521). The ring-shaped variant sold in Singapore as a soup/porridge accompaniment is not separately coded; closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and rolls as a category. A specific croissant ring roll sub-entry does not exist; category-level anchor used as the closest defensible code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for croissant ring roll; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 2 croissant ring rolls, approximately 70 g total, typically served alongside a bowl of soup or porridge.

nutrient_source.title: Croissant Ring Roll — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for Croissant Ring Roll - 2Pcs (cr_id G00010414). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central croissant reference (NDB 18007) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; croissant GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for a ring-shaped croissant roll. GI of 67 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain croissant (glucose reference = 100), which is the closest defensible analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 67 × (45.8 × 0.70) / 100 = 67 × 32.06 / 100 ≈ 21.5. Serving size used: 70 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High saturated fat (≈11.5 g/100 g), elevated sugar (11.26 g/100 g), and sodium at 300 mg/100 g with minimal potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit croissant ring roll to occasional consumption (≤1 piece) due to its high saturated fat content promoting arterial stiffness, its sugar load driving insulin-mediated sodium retention, and its 300 mg/100 g sodium contributing meaningfully to the daily 2000 mg ceiling recommended by HPB Singapore.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (45.8 g/100 g), elevated sugar (11.26 g/100 g), low dietary fibre (2.6 g/100 g), and medium-high GI of 67 producing a glycemic load of ≈21.5 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit croissant ring rolls because the combination of refined flour, 11.26 g/100 g sugar, low fibre, and a GI of 67 produces a high per-serving glycemic load (≈21.5) that will cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, pair with a protein source and limit to half a serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat ≈11.5 g/100 g, trans fat ≈0.1 g/100 g, cholesterol 67 mg/100 g, and high sugar 11.26 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat croissant ring rolls as an occasional indulgence only, as the high saturated fat raises LDL cholesterol, the trace trans fat is independently atherogenic, and the elevated sugar and refined carbohydrate load promote triglyceride synthesis and endothelial inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat ≈0.1 g/100 g (hard pro-inflammatory gate), saturated fat ≈11.5 g/100 g, and sugar 11.26 g/100 g promoting inflammatory cytokines
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid croissant ring rolls because even trace trans fat from butter/margarine is a hard pro-inflammatory disqualifier, and the high saturated fat and sugar content further upregulate TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory mediators relevant to joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Croissant Ring Roll (2 pieces) is a Singapore-market baked pastry product consisting of two ring-shaped croissant rolls made from laminated wheat dough enriched with butter, sugar, eggs, and salt. The product is sold as a bread accompaniment intended to be dipped into or mixed with soup or porridge, a common eating pattern in Singaporean hawker and café culture. The texture is flaky and layered on the outside with a soft, slightly chewy interior, and the flavour is mildly buttery and sweet. Per 100 g, the product delivers 406 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 21 g fat (of which approximately 11.5 g is saturated fat and 0.1 g is trans fat), 45.8 g carbohydrate, 11.26 g sugar, 2.6 g dietary fibre, and 300 mg sodium. The glycemic index is approximately 67 (medium), producing a glycemic load of about 21.5 per 70 g serving. Clinically, this product is high concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to its refined carbohydrate and sugar load with low fibre; high concern for hypertension due to saturated fat, sugar, and sodium; and high concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol. It is not suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the presence of trans fat. The product is vegetarian but not vegan, not certified halal, and contains gluten, dairy, and egg allergens. It is classified as ultra-processed. Nutritional data sourced from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id G00010414, confidence 0.95).
common_user_queries: What is a croissant ring roll?
How many calories in 2 croissant rolls?
Is croissant ring roll suitable for diabetics?
Can I eat croissant with porridge?
Is croissant ring roll high in sodium?
Croissant ring roll nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id G00010414) with a recorded confidence of 0.95 and review_status of reviewed. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from the USDA FoodData Central croissant reference (NDB 18007) as HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this item; this adaptation is documented in notes and nutrient_source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database plain croissant entry, which is the closest approved-source match.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (11.5 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (5.6 g/100 g), polyunsaturated fat (1.2 g/100 g), and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for croissant, butter (NDB 18007), as the HPB Foodo source for this item does not separately report fatty acid fractions. These values are consistent with the total fat of 21.0 g/100 g reported by HPB. 2. Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB Foodo or USDA for this specific product): vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for G00010414 and are not available in the USDA FoodData Central croissant entry at the level of specificity required for this Singapore-market ring-roll variant. 3. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database plain croissant entry; no direct GI measurement exists for a ring-shaped croissant roll. 4. The input food_subgroup was listed as 'Rice-based dishes', which is incorrect for a wheat-flour croissant product; corrected to 'Bread and bread products'. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; the product contains butter and eggs whose halal certification is not stated in the HPB record. 6. The description in the input ('To be mix into your bowl of Soup/Porridge') has been preserved in the LLM context as cultural usage context.
